title: Why do some starburst galaxies mysteriously shut down? New study provides clues
summary: A new study reveals that post-starburst galaxies stop forming stars primarily due to fuel depletion, though other factors may also play a role.

title: Watch Russia launch 3 tons of cargo to the International Space Station today
summary: Russia is set to launch its latest Progress cargo ship, carrying 3 tons of supplies, to the International Space Station on April 25. The event can be watched live.

title: How did humanity end up underground in 'Silo'? Apple TV digs deep in new Season 3 teaser (video)
summary: Apple TV released a teaser for Season 3 of 'Silo,' exploring the origins of humanity's underground existence. The show delves into the mystery behind the dystopian setting.

title: This X-ray image shows our solar system 'breathing'
summary: The eROSITA spacecraft has created the most precise map of soft X-rays over Earth, revealing a phenomenon where the solar system 'breathes' due to solar winds from the sun.

title: Viltrox AF 50mm f/1.4 Pro FE lens review
summary: The Viltrox AF 50mm f/1.4 Pro FE lens is a fast standard prime designed for astrophotography, offering competitive pricing.

title: How did the USSR win the space race to beat the US to the moon in Apple TV's new 'Star City'?
summary: Apple TV's new series 'Star City' explores how the USSR led the space race, including their early lead in lunar missions. The show highlights a Soviet cosmonaut's dedication to communism and his nation's achievements.

title: Comet PanSTARRS approaches Earth on April 26. Here's how to catch it in satellite imagery this weekend
summary: Comet PanSTARRS will approach Earth on April 26 and may be visible in satellite imagery this weekend. The comet recently survived a close encounter with the Sun.

title: Famous asteroid Ryugu may have been bombarded by a swarm of tiny space rocks 1,000 years ago
summary: The asteroid Ryugu may have been bombarded by a swarm of tiny space rocks around 1,000 years ago. Samples collected by Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft reveal evidence of this ancient impact.

title: 'Guess I'll be waiting a minute to surf again': Artemis 2 moon astronaut Christina Koch shares her recovery journey
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch shared her recovery journey after the Artemis 2 moon mission, noting difficulties in walking straight during a blindfold test as part of readjusting to Earth's gravity.

title: A Lego 'Project Hail Mary' set just soared into the stratosphere, breaking the Guinness World Record for highest flight
summary: A Lego model of the 'Project Hail Mary' spaceship reached the stratosphere, setting a Guinness World Record for the highest flight. The achievement highlights creativity and innovation in space-themed play.

title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
summary: Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager, reflects on his journey from watching Apollo missions as a child to participating in NASA's Artemis II mission in April 2026. He now plays a key role in enabling communications for astronauts journeying around the Moon.

title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, 5,000 light-years from Earth, to celebrate the mission's 36th launch anniversary on April 24, 2026.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway will answer STEM questions from Missouri students during a live Earth-to-space call aboard the International Space Station on April 30. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: NASA Celebrates Decade of University Innovation in Aeronautics
summary: NASA's University Leadership Initiative (ULI) has celebrated a decade of fostering aeronautical innovation and workforce development, supporting over 1,100 students across 100 schools.

title: Johnson Leaders Honored by National Space Club & Foundation
summary: Two leaders from NASA's Johnson Space Center were honored by the National Space Club & Foundation for their contributions to human spaceflight. Howard Hu received the Norman L. Baker Astronautics Engineer Award for his sustained technical contributions.

title: NASA Shares SpaceX Crew-13 Assignments for Space Station Mission
summary: NASA has announced the crew assignments for the SpaceX Crew-13 mission to the International Space Station, including astronauts from NASA and CSA. The mission is scheduled to launch no earlier than mid-September for a long-duration science expedition.

title: NASA Kennedy Prepares Facility for Roman Space Telescope Arrival
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center is preparing facilities for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for as early as September on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket. The telescope aims to deliver unprecedented deep, panoramic views of the cosmos to advance our understanding of the universe.

title: NASA Welcomes Jordan as 63rd Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Jordan signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 63rd signatory to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Jordan as the newest signatory during a ceremony at NASA headquarters.

title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
summary: NASA is developing technology to produce IV fluid in space, which could be crucial for medical emergencies during long-duration missions beyond low Earth orbit. This innovation aims to reduce reliance on pre-packed supplies and enhance crew safety.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the first flight of Block 3 of Starship at the Cape, with hopes to launch this year. The company continues to advance preparations for the upcoming mission.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to an OIG report highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of the spacesuit development.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven missions involving four countries and five launch vehicles are scheduled for the week of April 20, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches. This marks a busy period for space activities across multiple organizations.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused its booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable orbital launch vehicles.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a new Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality for future commercial space station operations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This marks a significant milestone for NASA's upcoming missions.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew experienced vehicle departures and conducted a spacewalk in March. The month was marked by significant activity aboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent CRS NG-23 mission completion.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the news, but no specific details about the space industry news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates significant milestones in the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites. This innovation aims to improve safety and efficiency in space operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on 8–9 June in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April–May. The event aims to gather feedback from smaller enterprises on space sector challenges and opportunities.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received at an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the project.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or efficiency in directional control. It highlights innovations that could enhance mission capabilities or reduce operational costs.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials efficiently in microgravity, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to drive scale and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The company is targeting the 'Golden Dome' as part of this strategy.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Former President Trump has appointed two space industry executives to key military space roles. The nominees require Senate confirmation before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs. The study is presented in a Payload Pro article.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has secured €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, arguing that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet the directives set by President Donald Trump. This marks another instance of bipartisan pushback against reductions in NASA's funding.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and explores the idea of the Navy reciprocating by tracking satellites from the sea.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has raised €25.7 million in a Series A funding round. The company plans to use the funds to advance its commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space cargo services.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is a missile defense program aiming to protect the US. Companies are competing to secure contracts for this ambitious initiative.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under its CLPS program, creating opportunities for various space industry players beyond just lunar-focused companies.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communication capabilities. The partnership is formed with Ukrainian firm Stetman to advance this initiative.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has unveiled its next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP) designed for extended spacecraft missions. This new component expands the company's offerings in satellite navigation technology.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected SpaceX and other operators' requests to share MSS spectrum bands, maintaining exclusive licensing rights. The decision was issued in an order on Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed with results. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver outcomes during this strategic delay.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy teleport sites at Equinx's data centers for its Meridian constellation. This partnership aims to support the ground infrastructure for SpinLaunch's satellite network.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by growth in IoT subscribers. CEO Matt Desch discussed plans for NTN direct launch and spectrum during the earnings call.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: French startup Univity secured a €27 million ($32 million) Series A funding round to develop two demonstration satellites for its Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: U.S. officials criticize the EU's proposed Space Act, calling it a complicated overreach. The act aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities within the EU.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed across 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a regional collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities across the Nordic region's space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen various sectors within the space industry.
